title: Release-Profil messen und entscheiden
goal: Die opt-level=z Entscheidung mit reproduzierbarer Messung und kurzer ADR bestätigen oder anpassen.

acceptance.assertion: Ein reproduzierbarer Benchmark vergleicht relevante Feedback- oder Replay-Pfade.
opt-level z, s und 3 wurden mit Größe und Laufzeit verglichen oder die Grenzen der Messung sind dokumentiert.
Eine kurze ADR hält die Profilentscheidung und ihre Prämissen fest.

metadata.queue_readiness_repair: 2026-07-08: current implementation ball; made ready and repo claim explicit so now lane is claim-safe.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in Heimlern PR #190: release-profile benchmark evidence compares opt-level z, s and 3 for Heimlern feedback/snapshot paths. ADR-0004 records the decision to change the workspace release profile from opt-level="z" to opt-level="s". Measured binary sizes were s=535680 bytes, z=538056 bytes and 3=579272 bytes; s was the smallest measured binary and faster than z on existing-slot feedback and snapshot replay, while 3 was faster but larger.
validation: Heimlern PR #190 GitHub CI passed: Analyze actions/python/rust, CodeQL, build-test, repo-root, schema-validate and templates.

Local before merge: cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p heimlern-bandits --example bench_feedback, cargo test, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, benchmark script py_compile, benchmark JSON parse, release-profile smoke and diff-check all passed.

Post-merge on origin/main 3ea32c3e62e4e5b348f19dbd30dbdfe8c098de53: cargo fmt --check, cargo test, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, benchmark script py_compile, benchmark JSON parse, release-profile smoke and diff-check all passed.

verified_acceptance.benchmark-added: bench_feedback is now reproducible and JSON-capable; scripts/benchmark_release_profiles.py builds isolated release-profile targets and measures existing-slot feedback, slot filling and snapshot replay.
verified_acceptance.profile-compared: opt-level z, s and 3 are compared in docs/benchmarks/release-profile-comparison-2026-07-08.json by binary size and runtime medians. s is smallest; 3 is fastest but larger; z is neither smallest nor fastest in the measured paths.
verified_acceptance.adr-recorded: docs/adr/0004-release-opt-level-s.md records the decision, benchmark command, measured results, rationale, consequences and non-claims.
